question: [File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getAccountFromAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger unbounded memory allocation by crafting a valid alias that matches many rows in the entity table, since entityFromAliasQuery has no LIMIT clause and getRows() fetches all matching rows into memory, causing the Node.js process to exhaust heap memory and crash the mirror node REST service?
